segments.text: When Rachel realized she was unable to give Jacob any children she was jealous of her sister. She complained to Jacob, {
I'll die if you don't give me children!
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Jacob became angry with Rachel and told her, {
Am I God? Do you think I'm the one stopping you having children?
segments.speaker: Jacob became angry with Rachel and

segments.text: Here's my personal maid Bilhah,
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Rachel replied.
Sleep with her and she can have children for me so I'll have a family too.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: She gave her personal maid Bilhah to him as a wife and Jacob slept with her.
Bilhah became pregnant and had a son for Jacob.
Rachel said, {
God has judged in my favor! He listened to me and gave me a son.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: So she named him Dan.
Rachel's personal maid Bilhah became pregnant again and had a second son for Jacob.
Rachel said, {
I've had a hard struggle with my sister, but I've won.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: So she named him Naphtali.
Leah realized she wasn't having any more children, so she gave her personal maid Zilpah to Jacob as a wife.
Zilpah had a son for Jacob.
Leah said, {
I'm really fortunate!
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: So she named him Gad.
Leah's personal maid Zilpah became pregnant again and had a second son for Jacob.
Leah said, {
I'm so happy, and the other women will say I'm happy too!
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: So she named him Asher.
At the time of the wheat harvest Reuben found some mandrake plants when he was out in the fields. He took them back to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah, {
Please give me some of the mandrakes your son found.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Aren't you satisfied with stealing my husband?
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: Leah replied.
Are you going to take my son's mandrakes too?
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: Fine, he can sleep with you tonight if you give me some mandrakes in return,
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Rachel responded.
When Jacob came in from the fields that evening, Leah went out to meet him. {
You have to sleep with me because I've paid for you with my son's mandrakes,
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: she told him. So he slept with her that night.
God heard Leah's request, and she became pregnant and had a fifth son for Jacob.
Leah said, {
The Lord has rewarded me for giving my personal maid to my husband.
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: So she named him Issachar.
Then Leah became pregnant again and had a sixth son for Jacob.
Leah said, {
God has given me a good gift. Now my husband will honor me because I've given him six sons.
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: So she named him Zebulun.
Later she had a daughter she named Dinah.
Then God paid attention to Rachel and listened to her appeals, and helped her to have children.
She became pregnant and had a son. {
God has removed my disgrace,
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: she said.
She named him Joseph,  saying, {
May the Lord give me an additional son.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Once Rachel had given birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, {
Let me leave so I can return to my home and my own country.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Give me my wives and children because I worked for you to have them. Let me go now because you know very well how much work I've done for you.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Please be so kind as to stay,
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Laban replied,
because I have discovered that the Lord has blessed me because of you.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Then Laban continued, {
Tell me how much to pay you and I'll give it to you.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: You certainly know how much work I've done for you, and how well your flocks have done under my care.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: You hardly had anything before I arrived, but now you have so much! The Lord has blessed you through what I've done. When am I going to be able to provide for my own family?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Well, what do you propose I give you?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Laban asked again.
You don't have to give me anything,
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Jacob replied.
If you want to do something for me, then how about this: I'll go on looking after your flocks, making sure they're fed.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Let me go through your flocks today and take all the sheep that are speckled or spotted, and all the dark ones, as well as all the speckled and spotted goats. They'll be my wages.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: In the future you'll be able to prove that I've been honest. When you check my flock, any goats that don't have speckles or spots, or any sheep that aren't dark will be considered stolen from you.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Very good,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Laban agreed.
We'll do as you say.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: However, the same day Laban went and removed all the striped and spotted male goats, all the speckled and spotted female goats, and all the dark sheep. He had his sons look after them and sent them away—
a three day journey between them and Jacob, while Jacob was looking after the rest of Laban's flocks.
Then Jacob cut some sticks from poplar, almond, and plane trees that had white wood under the bark. He peeled off some of the bark, making the sticks look streaked with white.
He put the sticks he'd peeled in the water troughs where the flocks came to drink because that's where they mated.
The flocks mated in front of the sticks and gave birth to young that were streaked, speckled, and spotted.
Jacob separated all these ones out. Then he made his flock face towards those in Laban's flock that were streaked and dark. This is the way he kept his flocks apart from Laban's flock.
When the strong females were ready to breed, Jacob put the sticks in the troughs where the flocks could see them when they mated.
He didn't do this for the weaker females. The weaker ones went to Laban, and the strong ones went to Jacob.
In this way Jacob became an extremely rich man with large flocks, and many male and female slaves, camels, and donkeys.
